nextToken.description: If Deadline Cloud returns nextToken, then there are more results available. The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page, call the operation again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. If no results remain, then nextToken is set to null. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours. If you provide a token that isn't valid, then you receive an HTTP 400 ValidationException error.

monitors.description: A list of MonitorSummary objects that describe your monitors in the Deadline Cloud.

usageTrackingResource.description: The resource that the budget is tracking usage for.

status.description:
The status of the budget.

  • ACTIVE–Get a budget being evaluated.

  • INACTIVE–Get an inactive budget. This can include expired, canceled, or deleted statuses.

displayName.description:
The display name of the budget.

statusMessage.description: A message that communicates a suspended status of the fleet.

autoScalingStatus.description: The Auto Scaling status of the fleet. Either GROWING, STEADY, or SHRINKING.

targetWorkerCount.description: The number of target workers in the fleet.

workerCount.description: The number of workers in the fleet.

minWorkerCount.description: The minimum number of workers specified in the fleet.

maxWorkerCount.description: The maximum number of workers specified in the fleet.

hostConfiguration.description: The script that runs as a worker is starting up that you can use to provide additional configuration for workers in your fleet.

capabilities.description: Outlines what the fleet is capable of for minimums, maximums, and naming, in addition to attribute names and values.

lifecycleStatus.description: The life cycle status for the job.

lifecycleStatusMessage.description: A message that communicates the status of the life cycle for the job.

createdAt.description: The date and time the resource was created.

createdBy.description: The user or system that created this resource.

updatedAt.description: The date and time the resource was updated.

updatedBy.description: The user or system that updated this resource.

startedAt.description: The date and time the resource started running.

endedAt.description: The date and time the resource ended running.

taskRunStatus.description: The task run status for the job.

targetTaskRunStatus.description: The task status with which the job started.

taskRunStatusCounts.description: The number of tasks running on the job.

taskFailureRetryCount.description: The total number of times tasks from the job failed and were retried.

storageProfileId.description: The storage profile ID associated with the job.

maxFailedTasksCount.description: The number of task failures before the job stops running and is marked as FAILED.

maxRetriesPerTask.description: The maximum number of retries per failed tasks.

description.description:
The description of the job.

This field can store any content. Escape or encode this content before displaying it on a webpage or any other system that might interpret the content of this field.

maxWorkerCount.description:
The maximum number of worker hosts that can concurrently process a job. When the maxWorkerCount is reached, no more workers will be assigned to process the job, even if the fleets assigned to the job's queue has available workers.

If you don't set the maxWorkerCount when you create a job, this value is not returned in the response.

sourceJobId.description: The job ID for the source job.

statusMessage.description: The status message of the license endpoint.

vpcId.description: The VCP(virtual private cloud) ID associated with the license endpoint.

securityGroupIds.description: The security group IDs for the license endpoint.

displayName.description:
The display name of the limit.

This field can store any content. Escape or encode this content before displaying it on a webpage or any other system that might interpret the content of this field.

amountRequirementName.description: The value that you specify as the name in the amounts field of the hostRequirements in a step of a job template to declare the limit requirement.

maxCount.description:
The maximum number of resources constrained by this limit. When all of the resources are in use, steps that require the limit won't be scheduled until the resource is available.

The maxValue must not be 0. If the value is -1, there is no restriction on the number of resources that can be acquired for this limit.

createdAt.description: The Unix timestamp of the date and time that the limit was created.

createdBy.description: The user identifier of the person that created the limit.

updatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp of the date and time that the limit was last updated.

updatedBy.description: The user identifier of the person that last updated the limit.

farmId.description: The unique identifier of the farm that contains the limit.

currentCount.description: The number of resources from the limit that are being used by jobs. The result is delayed and may not be the count at the time that you called the operation.

description.description:
The description of the limit that helps identify what the limit is used for.

displayName.description:
The name used to identify the monitor on the Deadline Cloud console.

This field can store any content. Escape or encode this content before displaying it on a webpage or any other system that might interpret the content of this field.

subdomain.description: The subdomain used for the monitor URL. The full URL of the monitor is subdomain.Region.deadlinecloud.amazonaws.com.

url.description: The complete URL of the monitor. The full URL of the monitor is subdomain.Region.deadlinecloud.amazonaws.com.

roleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role for the monitor. Users of the monitor use this role to access Deadline Cloud resources.

identityCenterInstanceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center instance responsible for authenticating monitor users.

identityCenterApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that the IAM Identity Center assigned to the monitor when it was created.

createdAt.description: The UNIX timestamp of the date and time that the monitor was created.

createdBy.description: The user name of the person that created the monitor.

updatedAt.description: The UNIX timestamp of the last date and time that the monitor was updated.

updatedBy.description: The user name of the person that last updated the monitor.

status.description:
The status of the queue.

  • ACTIVE–The queue is active.

  • SCHEDULING–The queue is scheduling.

  • SCHEDULING_BLOCKED–The queue scheduling is blocked. See the provided reason.

defaultBudgetAction.description: The default action taken on a queue if a budget wasn't configured.

jobAttachmentSettings.description: The job attachment settings for the queue.

requiredFileSystemLocationNames.description: A list of the required file system location names in the queue.

allowedStorageProfileIds.description: The storage profile IDs for the queue.

jobRunAsUser.description: The jobs in the queue ran as this specified POSIX user.

endedAt.description: The date and time the resource ended running.

workerUpdatedAt.description: The Linux timestamp of the date and time the session action was last updated.

progressPercent.description: The percentage completed for a session action.

sessionId.description: The session ID for the session action.

processExitCode.description: The process exit code. The default Deadline Cloud worker agent converts unsigned 32-bit exit codes to signed 32-bit exit codes.

progressMessage.description: The message that communicates the progress of the session action.

acquiredLimits.description: The limits and their amounts acquired during a session action. If no limits were acquired during the session, this field isn't returned.

manifests.description: The list of manifest properties that describe file attachments for the task run.

statistics.description: The statistics for the specified fleets or queues.

nextToken.description: If Deadline Cloud returns nextToken, then there are more results available. The value of nextToken is a unique pagination token for each page. To retrieve the next page, call the operation again using the returned token. Keep all other arguments unchanged. If no results remain, then nextToken is set to null. Each pagination token expires after 24 hours. If you provide a token that isn't valid, then you receive an HTTP 400 ValidationException error.

status.description:
The status of the aggregated results. An aggregation may fail or time out if the results are too large. If this happens, you can call the StartSessionsStatisticsAggregation operation after you reduce the aggregation time frame, reduce the number of queues or fleets in the aggregation, or increase the period length.

If you call the StartSessionsStatisticsAggregation operation when the status is IN_PROGRESS, you will receive a ThrottlingException.
